description: Info-theoretic learnability audit of the manufactured corpus (src/data/corpus_audit.py): for the two categorical targets the world model owns - outcome (status head) and error_class (error head) - compute label balance, majority-class baseline accuracy, marginal entropy H(Y), conditional entropy H(Y|op) given the edit op, and mutual information I(op;Y). A CPU-only precondition check run over all shards. Also hardens shard_stats directory mode to skip macOS AppleDouble (._*) sidecars that crashed the post-build auto-audit.
motivation: Before spending any GPU (e-0002), check the necessary condition for q-0005/q-0010: if the action carries ~0 information about the outcome and one class dominates, the status/error heads cannot beat a constant predictor at any model size.
formal_results: On the 66-record fixture corpus (6 decls, 3 shards): outcome distribution solved 47.0% / error 43.9% / still_valid 9.1%, majority-baseline acc 0.470, H=1.348 bits, H(Y|op)=0.760, I(op;Y)=0.588 bits. error_class: none 56.1% / other 22.7% / unsolved 18.2% / unknown_ident 1.5% / type_mismatch 1.5%, majority-baseline acc 0.561, H=1.584 bits, H(Y|op)=0.963, I(op;Y)=0.621 bits. 108 tests pass (+7).
results_description: Both heads clear the necessary precondition: non-degenerate marginals (no class >57%) and strictly positive action->target mutual information (~0.59-0.62 bits). The edit op alone already cuts target uncertainty by ~40% before any state context is read.
conclusions: Necessary (not sufficient) learnability condition is met at fixture scale: the manufactured labels are balanced enough and the action is informative enough that the WM status/error heads can in principle beat the majority baseline. Green light for e-0002; needs re-checking at Mathlib scale.
